--- title: "Green Jobs and Growth" subtitle: "Making the West of England the home for green jobs and growth" author: "SC" date: last-modified execute: echo: true warning: false message: false --- ## Context This chapter tracks indicators related to green economy and environmental sustainability across the West of England. Up to 10 indicators can be included, covering metrics like green jobs, renewable energy, carbon emissions, electric vehicle uptake, and green business growth. ## Setup ::: {.cell} ::: --- ## Indicator 1: Greenhouse Gas Emissions **Data source:** Department for Energy Security and Net Zero (DESNZ) - [Sub-national GHG emissions](https://www.gov.uk/government/collections/uk-territorial-greenhouse-gas-emissions-statistics)
**Last updated:** 2026-04-07
**Refresh schedule:** Annual
**Indicator ID:** RI_5_ghg_emissions
**Period covered:** 2023
**Indicator value:** 4410 Kt CO2e
**Change since last period:** -3.7%
### Overview Regional GHG emissions from transport, domestic, commercial, industrial and public sectors. This tracks the region's progress toward the net zero target by 2030 and identifies key sectors for emissions reduction. ::: {.cell} ```{.r .cell-code code-summary="RI_5_ghg_emissions analysis"} # Your code here RI_5_plot ``` ::: {.cell-output-display} ![](index_files/figure-html/RI_5_ghg_emissions-1.png){width=672} ::: ```{.r .cell-code code-summary="RI_5_ghg_emissions analysis"} # See chapters/01-economy/index.qmd for a worked example pattern: # 1. Load data using load_csv() # 2. Process/filter/aggregate data # 3. Create visualization with theme_weca() # 4. Calculate key statistics using format_number() ``` ::: ### Findings Emissions have declined steadily over several years: -26.1% since 2014. Transport and domestic sectors dominate. The region is currently not on track to meet the 2030 net zero target, indicating a need for accelerated action in key sectors. Demand reduction for personal car use and the introduction of low - carbon heating for homes remain key priorities for the region. --- ## Indicator 2: [Your Green Economy Indicator] **Data source:** **Last updated:** **Refresh schedule:** [Repeat structure - add up to 10 indicators total] --- ## Summary [Summary of key findings across all green economy indicators.
